era: 1990-1994. Metal/Grunge. E=8. Capitol Hill, Seattle. Roadrunner Records. Two albums. Metal peak of the grunge era heavy end.
members: Ben McMillan (vocals, born 1966, died January 20 2008, Seattle -- complications from type 1 diabetes)
key_albums: Push (1992, Roadrunner) -- debut, Tribe, Above Me
Inside Yours (1993, Roadrunner) -- more compositionally varied, retrospectively stronger
mcmillan_memorial: Ben McMillan, 1966-2008. Died January 20, 2008, Seattle. Complications from type 1 diabetes. Age 41. The engine documents with respect. The music stands.

theory_nodes.concept: SECOND STEP OF FINAL ASCENT. E=8 holds at degrees 341 (Pearl Jam) and 342 (Gruntruck) before rising to E=9 (343, Blood Brothers) and E=10 (344, Botch, FIRST IN ENGINE HISTORY). Two consecutive Capitol Hill E=8 bands of completely different scale and career span.
theory_nodes.description: E=8 base holds for two degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: Skin Yard lineage: Ben McMillan was Skin Yard vocalist (1986-1992, degree 338 band). Gruntruck is the McMillan project post-Skin Yard. The Capitol Hill metal continuum: Skin Yard (Deep Six 1986) → Gruntruck (1990). Both at E=8 in the engine at different degrees.
theory_nodes.description: Skin Yard to Gruntruck is the direct McMillan lineage.

theory_nodes.concept: Ben McMillan: born 1966, died January 20 2008, Seattle. Cause: complications from type 1 diabetes. Age 41. Vocalist Skin Yard 1986-1992, Gruntruck 1990-1994, Swallow (solo, mid-1990s). Voice: powerful mid-range instrument, between tenor scream and baritone grunge. Documented once, with respect.

Roadrunner Records placement (1992): appropriate for Gruntruck's actual sonic character. Roadrunner roster also included Sepultura, Fear Factory. Gruntruck was genuinely metal in a way most Seattle grunge was not. The label placement correctly identifies the band's genre position.

cross_references.relationship: Direct predecessor. Ben McMillan was Skin Yard's vocalist before Gruntruck. The Capitol Hill metal lineage runs directly from Skin Yard (degree 338) to Gruntruck (degree 342), with McMillan as the through-line.

Consecutive Capitol Hill E=8 bands. Pearl Jam: 35+ years, 13 million albums, still making records. Gruntruck: 4 years, two albums, 1994 dissolution. Same neighborhood, same energy, completely different scale. Both documented equally.
E=8 rises to E=9 at the next degree. The final ascent's first energy step. Blood Brothers bring post-HC/metal intensity that lifts the ramp.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=8 + E=8 = E=16. Gruntruck's collective metal peak (four musicians at maximum metal commitment in adverse commercial conditions) paired with the sea lion rookery in rain and wind (colony-scale roaring against the storm). Both: collective energy asserted against adverse conditions.
